name: Red Lotus Lake
description: Stunning wetlands ablaze with pink lotus flowers.

name: Phraya Nakhon Cave
description: At the right time of day, sunlight floods through the roof of this cave and illuminates the small pavilion hidden within.

name: Wat Rong Khun
description: A white Buddhist temple with a golden restroom.

name: Half-Century-Old Soup
description: At a Bangkok bistro, one pot of beef stew has been cooking non-stop for more than 45 years.

name: Wat Samphran Temple
description: An enormous dragon scales the walls of this 17-story temple.

name: Amulet Market
description: Bangkok's men with dangerous jobs come here in search of protective talismans.

name: Erawan Cascading Waterfalls and Caves
description: A limestone wonderland of water and subterranean secrets.

name: The Wang Saen Suk Hell Garden
description: Walk through depictions of the torture inflicted on those who go to Buddhist hell.

name: Papaya Studio
description: Vintage furniture and art share space with all manner of collectibles at this quirky shop in Bangkok.

name: Treepod Restaurant
description: Eat like a bird in a bamboo nest.

name: Ko Ta Pu (James Bond Island)
description: A tall karst islet popularized by 007.

name: Maeklong Railway Market
description: Six times a day a train runs right through the middle of this Thai street market, sending vendors scrambling.

name: Elephant Nature Park
description: Thailand's distressed elephants find peace at this Chiang Mai sanctuary.

name: Siriraj Medical Museum
description: A treasure trove of pathological, forensic, parasitological, and anatomical specimens.

name: Erawan Museum
description: This museum has a most unusual location: inside a giant, three-headed elephant.

name: Bua Tong Sticky Waterfall
description: You can walk up Thailand’s naturally non-slip waterfall.

name: Wat Pha Sorn Kaew (Temple on the Glass Cliff)
description: Modern design and unusual architecture make this mountaintop temple no ordinary Buddhist retreat.

name: Wat Mahathat (Temple of the Great Relic)
description: The headquarters of the Mahanikai school of Buddhism, Thailand's largest monastic order.

name: Wat Arun
description: This dazzling Buddhist temple was constructed from over one million ceramic tiles salvaged from a British shipwreck.

name: Cabbages & Condoms
description: A condom-covered Thai restaurant that is more concerned with birth control than food.

name: Haoma
description: An on-site urban farm and fish pond supply some of the produce for this upscale restaurant.

name: Sanctuary of Truth
description: A modern temple with an ancient philosophy.

name: Phra Prang Sam Yot
description: Monkeys lounge around at this 13th century temple.

name: Banphasawan
description: From dragonfruit to mangoes to durian this Thailand resort allows guests to stay inside all manner of giant fruit.

name: Queen Saovabha Memorial Institute
description: A collection of venomous snakes and reptiles for the whole family to enjoy.

name: Nightingale-Olympic
description: Time-frozen department store of dangerous and dusty relics.

name: Baan Dam (Black House)
description: Where an entire elephant skeleton, a snakeskin table runner, and thousands of animal remains become art.

name: Wat Tham Pla
description: A Buddhist shrine in a cave protected by macaques next to dark passages lined with creepy paintings.

name: Jok's Kitchen
description: A three-table eatery sits inside the home-kitchen of a seafood virtuoso in Bangkok's Chinatown.

name: Thailand's Biggest Buddha
description: The golden Buddha statue at the Wat Muang Monastery is the tallest in Thailand, and the ninth-tallest in the world.

name: Wat Saam Prasob, the Sunken Temple
description: During the rainy season, this temple is almost entirely submerged, accessible only by boat.

name: Wat Tha Ka Rong
description: A colorful kitschy temple and floating market off the beaten path.

name: Pha Nang Khoi Cave
description: The long underground cave's curiously shaped stalagmites have inspired many legends.

name: The World Museum of Insects and Natural Wonders
description: This extensive collection of dead bugs may be the world's most idiosyncratic and personal insect museum.

name: Wat Umong
description: Buddha shrines hide within dark tunnels at this unique forest temple.

name: Giant Swing
description: For centuries young Thai men used this Giant Swing as a slingshot to heaven.

name: Rafflesia
description: Surat Thani's official flower—and the world's largest—smells of rotting meat.

name: The Abandoned Bangkok Mansions
description: Abandoned mini-mansions that were once intended for affluent Bangkok families now sit empty.

name: Pai Canyon
description: Bizarre dirt trail winds a narrow path above the tree line.

name: Elephant Building
description: This Bangkok high-rise looks like a rudimentary LEGO elephant.

name: Bat Caves of Khao Yai National Park
description: Thousands of bats stream out of caves every night on the edge on Thailand's second largest park.

name: Counterfeit Goods Museum
description: A museum in a law office, filled with authentic and counterfeit goods.

name: Mummy of Luang Pho Dang
description: Self-mummified monk who wears sunglasses and has become a gecko hatchery.

name: Ko Hin-Ngam
description: The cursed island of black pebbles.

name: Bo Sang Umbrella Village
description: Peek into the world of those creating the beautiful, delicate paper parasols found all over the globe.

name: Sala Keoku
description: Bizarre Hindu and Buddhist sculpture park.

name: Erawan Shrine
description: A shrine built to combat the bad karma of a commercial venture.

name: Wat Pa Thewapithak
description: The skeletons, demons, and corpses serve as a warning of the terrors of Buddhist Hell.

name: Boon Ko Ku So Bridge
description: This serene bamboo walkway allows Buddhist monks from remote temples access to the nearby village.

name: Tham Lod Cave
description: A mile-long navigable cave adorned with stalactites and stalagmites reaching up to 65 feet tall.

name: Phaya Thaen Public Park
description: Its tremendous toad is actually a five-story museum and viewing platform.

name: Wat Pa Maha Chedi Kaew
description: Temple of a million beers.

name: Wat Tham Ta Pan
description: Grotesque statues of figures from Buddhist hell haunt the temple's Sin Park.

name: Robot Building
description: The bank's whimsical shape was inspired by a child’s toy.

name: Wat Mahabut
description: A shrine honoring a legendary ghost bride lies within this vibrant temple.

name: Phu Chi Fah
description: This mountain sunrise is one of the most breathtaking views in Thailand, yet well off the tourist trail.

name: Samut Songkhram Salt Farms
description: Acres of salt crystal pyramids dry in the sun along Thailand's "salt road."

name: Wat Sa Kamphaeng Yai Hell Garden
description: Gruesome statues of people reborn in Buddhist hell stand among the ruins of an 11th-century sanctuary.

name: Chit Beer
description: On a river island north of Bangkok, a small bar helped lead a home-brewing rebellion.

name: Wat Phra Yai (Big Buddha Temple)
description: A dazzling temple featuring a towering 12-meter golden Buddha.

name: The Kingdom of Somchai’s Affection
description: On the small Thai island of Ko Mak, there is a forgotten garden of erotic statuary.

name: House of Opium
description: A small museum in the Golden Triangle tells the story of the opium drug trade that gripped Northern Thailand.

name: Abandoned King View Resort
description: A forsaken beachfront condo abandoned after the Boxing Day Tsunami.

name: White Buddha Temple
description: A meditative walk up 353 stairs leads to a majestic white Buddha overlooking Pai.

name: Mahachulalongkornrajavidyalaya University
description: Buddhist university that allows English-language speakers the opportunity to study with monks in Thailand.

name: Phanom Rung Historical Park
description: This Khmer-era temple includes a lintel that was stolen and displayed at the Art Institute of Chicago for years before returning to Thailand.

name: Death Railway Bridge
description: This bridge made famous in a novel and on-screen wasn't actually built over the river Kwai.

name: Mini Siam
description: Cities of the world and famous Thai monuments in minature.

name: Elephant Poo Poo Paper Park
description: You'll never meet anyone this passionate about elephant droppings.

name: Phra Nang Cave
description: A phallic shrine to a princess goddess in Krabi, Thailand.

name: Krabi Traffic Lights
description: Cavemen, elephants, and eagles greet passengers driving through the town's main intersection.

name: Tham Tu Pu
description: This elegant carving of the Buddha guards a small Buddhist temple within a cave.

name: Wat Phumin
description: Riding on a pair of architectural dragons, this Thailand temple holds murals full of lurid imagery.

name: Democracy Monument
description: Bangkok celebrated its shifting monarchy with a militaristic homage to democracy.

name: Chicken Island
description: This nugget of paradise earned its name for obvious reasons.

name: Sahachat Memorial
description: This ambiguous pig shrine is actually a monument to a beloved queen.

name: Ancient Siam Miniature Park
description: All of Thailand's architectural landmarks, plus some made up ones, all viewable via two-seater bicycle.

name: Wat Yannawa Boat Temple
description: Founded during the Ayutthaya era, this unique Buddhist temple remains shipshape to this day.

name: The Elephant Kraal of Ayutthaya
description: This elephant kraal is the only one remaining in Thailand, dating back to the 16th century when these massive mammals were integral players in war and trade.

name: Solar Chicken
description: In Phetchaburi, Thailand, a roadside vendor cooks his fare with 1,000 mirrors and the power of the sun.

name: Wat Nong Bua Yai
description: An underwater temple submerged for 20 years has resurfaced with an extreme drought.

name: Phra Achana
description: An enormous Buddha peers through a gap in its ruined chamber.

name: Kid Mai Death Awareness Cafe
description: An immersive exhibition that meditates on death and life.

name: Buddha Head in Tree Roots
description: One of Ayutthaya’s most iconic locations is a mysterious, hauntingly surreal miracle.

name: Hai Som Tam Convent
description: Isaan street food staples are in spectacular form at this no-frills institution.

name: Gongs of the Phibun Mangsahan Village
description: Thailand's country villages continue the tradition of gong-making, and invite visitors to watch the process.

name: The Standing Buddha
description: This statue took 60 years to complete.

name: Secret Buddha Garden
description: A sculpture garden built by a retired durian farmer.

name: Kor Panich
description: Visit a century-old sweet shop serving gold-standard mango sticky rice.

name: Golden Buddha of Wat Traimit
description: This enormous gold Buddha statue was hidden inside a stucco decoy for centuries.

name: Phu Phra Bat Historical Park
description: An amalgam of precariously balanced boulders, ancient petroglyphs, and Buddhist shrines.

name: Chang and Eng Memorial Statue
description: Thailand's tribute to the original "Siamese Twins" Chang and Eng, in the town the famous pair was born in.

name: Bangkok Coin Museum
description: Chronicling the history of Thailand’s unique currencies, from the silver “bullet money” to porcelain gambling tokens of various shapes.

name: Wat Sri Suphan
description: This sensational silver temple is the product of centuries of metalworking.

name: Wat Phai Rong Wua
description: Parts of this sprawling temple complex are deteriorating, but its founder’s body remains intact.

name: Chao Phraya Sky Park
description: Bangkok's take on the High Line is a park on a traffic bridge above the iconic Chao Phraya River.

name: Doi Tung Trenches
description: The Thai Army has dug deep trenches along the Thai/Myanmar border to prevent drug smuggling.

name: Khao Chi Chan (Buddha Mountain)
description: Limestone outcrop decorated with a large laser engraving of the Buddha.

name: Sri Nakhon Khuean Khan Park
description: "Bangkok's Green Lung," a large park on an island, feels worlds away from traffic-choked Sukhumvit Road.

name: ChaTraMue
description: While it might look like an ordinary chain, this is the shop that turned Thai iced tea into a global phenomenon.

name: Gai Tord Jae Kee
description: Bangkok’s most beloved fried chicken spot lets its garlic-burrowed birds do the talking.

name: Wat Mok Khan Lan, Chom Thong Town (Temple of the Dinosaurs)
description: A unique Buddhist temple with strange statues, beehives, and dinosaurs.

name: City Pillar Shrine
description: The pillars inside this small, ornate shrine hold Bangkok's horoscope within them.

name: Air Diamond Cafe
description: In a strip mall parking lot, an airplane with a cafe inside, flanked by a giant statue of Fred Flintstone.

name: Tep Bar
description: Sidle up for this Chinatown watering hole for Thai moonshine accompanied by traditional live tunes.

name: Raya
description: Feast on coconut crab curry at one of Thailand’s only outposts of Baba cuisine.

name: Wat Pariwat Ratchasongkram
description: An eclectic Buddhist temple packed with pop-culture icons.

name: Wat Sutthi Wararam
description: Displaying religious art made almost exclusively in modern styles helps set this Buddhist temple apart from the myriad others in Bangkok.

name: Aksorn
description: Time-travel through Thai cuisine drawn from vintage cookbooks and little-known ingredients.

name: Ban Yipun (Ayutthaya Japanese Village)
description: The site of a Japanese traders’ settlement that once thrived in Ayutthaya, if for less than a century.

name: Charmgang
description: This neon-lit back-alley spot deals in good vibes and curries that pack a punch.

name: John Gallery The Tree House
description: A local Thai artist started building this gallery in 1980.

name: Gedhawa
description: For more than 30 years, this stalwart has been one of the few restaurants in Bangkok specializing in northern Thai cuisine.

name: Kanomwaan Pakee
description: Make a beeline to this rural outpost for one of Thailand’s rarest desserts.

name: Wat Pho Reclining Buddha
description: Striking, enormous gold-plated sculpture of Buddha installed in the 19th century.

name: Khanom Jeen Pa Mai
description: Tackle southern Thailand’s favorite dish at this hyper-casual Phuket Town restaurant.

name: Grandfather and Grandmother Rocks (Hin Ta Hin Yai)
description: This natural rock formation has an anatomical aesthetic.

name: Kai Thawt Bat Khiw
description: Crunchy, fragrant, and complex, Hat Yai-style fried chicken is renowned throughout Thailand—here’s where to get it in its origin city.

name: Rung Rueang
description: The porky tom yum soup at this Sukhumvit Road institution has a cult following.

name: Nai Ek Roll Noodle
description: This old-timey Chinatown institution specializes in one of the city’s best Thai-Chinese fusion dishes.

name: Nai Mong Hoi Thod
description: This Chinatown hole-in-the-wall serves what might be the ultimate oyster omelet.

name: Betong Chicken Rice
description: Try Thailand’s distinctive take on Hainanese chicken rice at the source.

name: Wat Sirindhorn Phu Phrao
description: Hundreds of glow-in-the-dark designs bring a Thai temple to life once the sun has set.

name: Prasat Nakhon Luang
description: These palatial ruins said to be inspired by Cambodia's Angkor Wat have been turned into a significant religious temple.

name: Nasi Dagae
description: Wake up early for 'trader's rice,' a highly regional Thai-Muslim specialty.

name: Suwalee
description: In southern Thailand's curry stall capital, this family-run institution serves an ever-changing array of fiery dishes.

name: Thai-Islam Phochana
description: Start your day with sticky rice sweets and aromatic curries at this classic Thai-Muslim breakfast spot.

name: Bangpor Seafood Takho
description: Dig into rustic southern Thai island dishes at this decades-old beach shack.

name: Yay Puad
description: Dine on southern Thai “fusion” cuisine in a home-based, second-generation restaurant.

name: Sin Jiew
description: Step back in time for Chinese specialties with a local twist at this food court in Trang.

name: Maksu Soup Chormalee
description: Slow-simmered beef soup pairs surprisingly perfectly with an omelet at this Thai-Muslim institution.

name: Wat Chai Sri
description: An early 1900s temple features unique architecture and murals.

name: Wat Maniwong
description: Get lost in the fantasy underworld of Thai serpents where wishes are granted

name: Bangkok Airplane Graveyard
description: Derelict airplane bodies serve as both home and income for three Thai families.

name: Bangkok's Ghost Tower: The Sathorn Unique
description: On stormy days debris from this unfinished and abandoned skyscraper rains down on the Bangkok streets.

name: Archipelago Cinema
description: This floating cinema brings new meaning to the phrase "going out" to the movies.

name: Goddess Tuptim Shrine
description: A forest of phallic offerings found in a quiet corner of a hotel car park.

name: Phone Booth Graveyard
description: This small patch of land is the final resting for these communication relics of the past.

name: The Bottle Art Museum
description: Thailand museum of bottles of all shapes and sizes filled with intricate and beautiful art.

name: Dragon Roundabout
description: This most unusual dragon is made of recycled Chinese cutlery.
